name: Plan with mixed valid and invalid token keys — tests pre-build validation
expectedBehaviors: Runs a pre-build validation pass over all tokenKey values in the plan before executing anything
Identifies that 'Colors/Text/text-primary', 'semantic/action/primary-bg', and 'Border/Default/border-subtle' are path-style keys, not 40-char hex variable IDs
Reports exactly which plan entries have bad keys and where they appear
Does NOT start creating frames or instantiating components until keys are resolved
Suggests running /setup-tokens to refresh the token map, or asks the user to supply correct keys
If user provides corrected keys, resumes build from where it left off
antiPatterns: Silently proceeds and tokens fail to bind — frames appear with wrong or missing colors
Crashes mid-build after creating some frames but not others, leaving Figma in a partial state
Treats path-style keys as valid and passes them to figma_update_variable or figma_set_fills
Only catches the first bad key and misses the others
Refuses to build entirely without offering a path forward (just errors out)
Validates keys by format only (length check) but doesn't verify they exist in the actual variable collection

evaluationCriteria.earlyDetection: Does it catch ALL bad keys before any Figma mutations happen?
evaluationCriteria.errorClarity: Does the error message list each bad key with its location in the plan?
evaluationCriteria.recoverySuggestion: Does it suggest /setup-tokens or another concrete fix, not just 'fix your keys'?
evaluationCriteria.atomicity: Does it avoid partial builds — either all-or-nothing, or clearly resumable?
evaluationCriteria.validKeyPass: Does it correctly accept the valid 40-char hex keys without false positives?
